title: Quiz: Version Control for Quantum Projects
description: Test your understanding of version control strategies for quantum software development

questions.question: Why is it important to record quantum hardware calibration timestamps alongside experiment results?

options.text: To track how long experiments take to run
Because calibration changes daily and affects circuit behavior, making results time-dependent
To comply with quantum computing regulations
Calibration timestamps are only needed for debugging

questions.explanation: Quantum hardware calibration (T1/T2 times, gate errors) changes frequently, often daily. A circuit that produces certain results today may behave differently tomorrow due to calibration drift. Recording timestamps is essential for reproducibility.

questions.question: Which file format is recommended for storing quantum circuits in version control for optimal code review?

options.text: QPY (Qiskit binary format) for fastest loading
Text-based formats like QASM or JSON for readable diffs
Pickle files for Python compatibility
HDF5 for compression

questions.explanation: Text-based formats like QASM and JSON produce readable diffs in version control, making code review possible. Binary formats like QPY or pickle cannot be meaningfully diffed and should be avoided for circuit definitions.

questions.question: What is 'experiment provenance' in quantum computing?

options.text: The country where the quantum computer is located
The complete record of all parameters, settings, and conditions needed to reproduce an experiment
The programming language used to write the quantum code
The cost of running the experiment

questions.explanation: Experiment provenance is the complete record of how an experiment was conducted, including circuit definitions, transpilation settings, backend calibration state, random seeds, and all parameters necessary to reproduce results.

questions.question: When should Git LFS be used in a quantum project?

options.text: For all Python source files
For large binary files like simulation results, calibration data, and NumPy arrays
Only for documentation files
Git LFS should never be used in quantum projects

questions.explanation: Git LFS should be used for large binary files that would bloat the repository, such as simulation results (.npy, .hdf5), serialized objects (.pkl), and calibration data. Text files like source code and QASM should be tracked normally.

questions.question: According to semantic versioning for quantum libraries, which change requires a MAJOR version bump?

options.text: Adding a new quantum algorithm implementation
Fixing a bug in circuit transpilation
Changing the parameter format from dict to ndarray (breaking API change)
Improving documentation

questions.explanation: A MAJOR version bump is required for breaking changes that affect backward compatibility, such as changing API interfaces, parameter formats, or gate decompositions that affect results. New features warrant MINOR bumps, and bug fixes warrant PATCH bumps.

questions.question: Which of the following should be included in a quantum experiment reproducibility record? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Git commit hash
Backend calibration timestamp
Random seed used
The researcher's lunch menu that day

questions.explanation: For reproducibility, you need the exact code version (git commit), hardware state (calibration timestamp), and all sources of randomness (random seed). The researcher's lunch is not relevant to experiment reproducibility.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'experiment/' branch prefix in quantum project branching strategy?

options.text: To store production code
To isolate research experiments that may or may not be merged
To track hardware-specific optimizations
To prepare releases

questions.explanation: The 'experiment/' prefix is used for research experiments and studies that explore new ideas. These branches may or may not be merged into the main development line, depending on whether the experiment is successful.

questions.question: Why should circuit hashes be computed and stored alongside version information?

options.text: To encrypt the circuit for security
To verify circuit integrity and detect if the circuit definition has changed
To compress the circuit for storage
Circuit hashes are only useful for debugging

questions.explanation: Circuit hashes provide a quick way to verify that a circuit definition hasn't changed. Even if version numbers match, comparing hashes can detect accidental modifications or confirm that two circuits are identical.
